figures.caption: Fig. 1: Problem Setup for PdM TSAD. During the training stage, clients train the local model and transmit shared weight ws c to the server for multiple rounds. The validation stage individually estimates personalized anomaly threshold ϵc for each client. Finally, during testing, the clients evaluate the performance on the new time series data.

Fig. 4: Time offset for different experimental setups: a) - Centralized Learning; b) - Independent Learning; c) Federated Learning with full model transmission; d) - Federated Learning with Encoder/Critic sharing; e) - Federated Learning with Decoder/Generator sharing. Blue color represents the average offset for the late detection; Green color represents average early detection offset while orange color shows the average absolute offset.

TABLE I: Performance comparison across Centralized, Independent, and Federated Learning settings. Table I(a) reports the ARAMIS benchmark, while Table I(b) reports the complementary cross-domain validation on the SWaT database. The results for Independent and Federated Learning settings are averaged over the clients.
TABLE II: Communication cost per client and round under 32-bit parameter transmission.

Fig. 3: Examples of the Aramis dataset for the selected use-case: a) - sensors operating under normal conditions throughout the simulation time T , b) - an example of an anomaly occurring after τ = 944 atu (red vertical line).
Fig. 2: A comparative overview of major generative model architectures: GANs, VAEs, and Diffusion Models.
